title: Cottage Cheese Taco Shells
tagline: The protein taco shell that ate TikTok this summer.
description: Blended cottage cheese and egg whites baked into flexible high-protein taco shells, then filled with seasoned ground chicken. Around 12g protein per shell before you add anything. The current cottage-cheese wave's best trick.
ingredients: To serve: shredded lettuce, pico de gallo, hot sauce, lime
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line two sheet pans with parchment and mist with oil - these shells stick with intent.
Blend cottage cheese, egg whites, garlic powder, paprika, and salt until completely smooth, about 30 seconds.
Pour into six 5-inch rounds, about 1/4 cup each, and spread thin and even with the back of a spoon.
Bake 20-25 minutes until set and golden at the edges with no wet shine in the center.

Meanwhile, cook the filling: heat oil over medium-high, brown the chicken, breaking it up, 5-6 minutes. Add spices and salt, stir 30 seconds, add water, and simmer 2-3 minutes until saucy. Chicken should hit 165°F.
Peel shells off the parchment while warm. To crisp them taco-shaped, drape each over two bars of the oven rack and bake 4-5 more minutes.
Fill and eat immediately - shells soften as they sit.
notes: Full-fat cottage cheese only. Nonfat weeps and turns rubbery.
Whole eggs make a sturdier, eggier shell; whites keep it lighter and more neutral.
These are wraps in taco cosplay - flexible when warm, crisp only if you rack-bake them.
testedNote: Rack-draping is what separates a taco from a sad fold - don't skip the second bake.

verdict.note: Legitimately good protein delivery - just don't expect a corn tortilla.

troubleshooting: Shells stuck → parchment wasn't oiled or peeled cold; grease the paper, peel warm.
Rubbery → nonfat cottage cheese or unblended lumps; full-fat, blended smooth.
Cracked when folded → overbaked or filled cold; fill while warm.
Watery filling → skipped the simmer; cook until the water reduces to a coating.
